topics.title: Cryptography
topics.definition: Cryptography is the science of protecting information by converting readable data into an unreadable format using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys. It ensures secure communication over insecure networks.
examPoints: Cryptography is the science of protecting information by converting readable data into an unreadable format using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys. It ensures secure communication over insecure networks.
topics.narration:
Cryptography

Cryptography is the science of protecting information by converting readable data into an unreadable format using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys. It ensures secure communication over insecure networks.

Important exam points. Cryptography is the science of protecting information by converting readable data into an unreadable format using mathematical algorithms and cryptographic keys. It ensures secure communication over insecure networks.. Goals: Confidentiality, Integrity, Authentication, Non-Repudiation. Types: Symmetric, Asymmetric, Hashing

topics.title: Encryption
topics.definition: Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and a key, making the data unreadable to unauthorized users.
examPoints: Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and a key, making the data unreadable to unauthorized users.
topics.narration:
Encryption

Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and a key, making the data unreadable to unauthorized users.

Important exam points. Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext using an algorithm and a key, making the data unreadable to unauthorized users.. Provides Confidentiality. Reverse process is Decryption

topics.title: Encryption Workflow
topics.definition: Encryption starts with plaintext, applies an encryption algorithm using a key to produce ciphertext. The receiver uses the correct key to decrypt the ciphertext back into plaintext.
examPoints: Encryption starts with plaintext, applies an encryption algorithm using a key to produce ciphertext. The receiver uses the correct key to decrypt the ciphertext back into plaintext.
topics.narration:
Encryption Workflow

Encryption starts with plaintext, applies an encryption algorithm using a key to produce ciphertext. The receiver uses the correct key to decrypt the ciphertext back into plaintext.

Important exam points. Encryption starts with plaintext, applies an encryption algorithm using a key to produce ciphertext. The receiver uses the correct key to decrypt the ciphertext back into plaintext.

topics.title: Components of Encryption
topics.definition: Every encryption system consists of plaintext, encryption algorithm, encryption key, ciphertext, decryption algorithm, and decryption key.
examPoints: Every encryption system consists of plaintext, encryption algorithm, encryption key, ciphertext, decryption algorithm, and decryption key.
topics.narration:
Components of Encryption

Every encryption system consists of plaintext, encryption algorithm, encryption key, ciphertext, decryption algorithm, and decryption key.

Important exam points. Every encryption system consists of plaintext, encryption algorithm, encryption key, ciphertext, decryption algorithm, and decryption key.. Remember:. Plaintext. Ciphertext. Algorithm. Key

topics.title: Encryption vs Encoding vs Hashing
comparisons: Encryption: Reversible; Encoding: Reversible; Hashing: Not Reversible
Encryption: Uses Key; Encoding: No Key; Hashing: No Decryption Key
Encryption: Security; Encoding: Representation; Hashing: Integrity
topics.narration:
Encryption vs Encoding vs Hashing

Comparison. Encryption: Reversible; Encoding: Reversible; Hashing: Not Reversible. Encryption: Uses Key; Encoding: No Key; Hashing: No Decryption Key. Encryption: Security; Encoding: Representation; Hashing: Integrity

topics.title: Classical Cryptography
topics.definition: Classical cryptography consists of traditional manual encryption techniques developed before modern computers.
examPoints: Classical cryptography consists of traditional manual encryption techniques developed before modern computers.
topics.narration:
Classical Cryptography

Classical cryptography consists of traditional manual encryption techniques developed before modern computers.

Important exam points. Classical cryptography consists of traditional manual encryption techniques developed before modern computers.. Examples:. Caesar. Vigenère. Playfair. Hill. Rail Fence. Vernam

topics.title: Caesar Cipher
topics.definition: A substitution cipher where every letter is shifted by a fixed number of positions.
examPoints: A substitution cipher where every letter is shifted by a fixed number of positions.
Easy to break using frequency analysis
topics.narration:
Caesar Cipher

A substitution cipher where every letter is shifted by a fixed number of positions.

Important exam points. A substitution cipher where every letter is shifted by a fixed number of positions.. Shift Cipher. Easy to break using frequency analysis

topics.title: Vigenère Cipher
topics.definition: A polyalphabetic substitution cipher that uses a keyword to encrypt plaintext.
examPoints: A polyalphabetic substitution cipher that uses a keyword to encrypt plaintext.
topics.narration:
Vigenère Cipher

A polyalphabetic substitution cipher that uses a keyword to encrypt plaintext.

Important exam points. A polyalphabetic substitution cipher that uses a keyword to encrypt plaintext.. More secure than Caesar. Uses repeating keyword

topics.title: Vernam Cipher
topics.definition: Encrypts data using XOR between plaintext and key.
examPoints: Encrypts data using XOR between plaintext and key.
If key is random, same length, used once → One-Time Pad
topics.narration:
Vernam Cipher

Encrypts data using XOR between plaintext and key.

Important exam points. Encrypts data using XOR between plaintext and key.. If key is random, same length, used once → One-Time Pad. Perfect secrecy (theoretical)

topics.title: Hill Cipher
topics.definition: Uses matrix multiplication to encrypt blocks of letters.
examPoints: Uses matrix multiplication to encrypt blocks of letters.
topics.narration:
Hill Cipher

Uses matrix multiplication to encrypt blocks of letters.

Important exam points. Uses matrix multiplication to encrypt blocks of letters.. Uses Linear Algebra. Block Cipher

topics.title: Playfair Cipher
topics.definition: Encrypts pairs of letters using a 5×5 matrix.
examPoints: Encrypts pairs of letters using a 5×5 matrix.
topics.narration:
Playfair Cipher

Encrypts pairs of letters using a 5×5 matrix.

Important exam points. Encrypts pairs of letters using a 5×5 matrix.. Encrypts digraphs. Removes J or combines I/J

topics.title: Rail Fence Cipher
topics.definition: A transposition cipher where characters are written diagonally and read row-wise.
examPoints: A transposition cipher where characters are written diagonally and read row-wise.
topics.narration:
Rail Fence Cipher

A transposition cipher where characters are written diagonally and read row-wise.

Important exam points. A transposition cipher where characters are written diagonally and read row-wise.. Rearranges letters. Does not substitute letters

topics.title: XOR Cipher
topics.definition: Uses XOR operation between plaintext and key.
examPoints: Uses XOR operation between plaintext and key.
XOR twice with same key → Original data
topics.narration:
XOR Cipher

Uses XOR operation between plaintext and key.

Important exam points. Uses XOR operation between plaintext and key.. XOR twice with same key → Original data. Widely used internally in cryptography

topics.title: Weakness of Classical Ciphers
topics.narration:
Weakness of Classical Ciphers

Important exam points. Frequency Analysis. Brute Force. Not Secure Today

topics.title: Symmetric Cryptography
topics.definition: Uses the same secret key for encryption and decryption.
examPoints: Uses the same secret key for encryption and decryption.
topics.narration:
Symmetric Cryptography

Uses the same secret key for encryption and decryption.

Important exam points. Uses the same secret key for encryption and decryption.. Fast. Suitable for large data. Key Distribution Problem

topics.title: Asymmetric Cryptography
topics.definition: Uses a Public Key and a Private Key for encryption and decryption.
examPoints: Uses a Public Key and a Private Key for encryption and decryption.
topics.narration:
Asymmetric Cryptography

Uses a Public Key and a Private Key for encryption and decryption.

Important exam points. Uses a Public Key and a Private Key for encryption and decryption.. Solves Key Distribution. Slow

topics.title: Symmetric vs Asymmetric
comparisons: Symmetric: One Key; Asymmetric: Two Keys
Symmetric: Bulk Data; Asymmetric: Key Exchange
topics.narration:
Symmetric vs Asymmetric

Comparison. Symmetric: One Key; Asymmetric: Two Keys. Symmetric: Fast; Asymmetric: Slow. Symmetric: Bulk Data; Asymmetric: Key Exchange. Symmetric: AES; Asymmetric: RSA

topics.title: DES
topics.definition: Data Encryption Standard is a symmetric block cipher using a 56-bit key.
examPoints: Data Encryption Standard is a symmetric block cipher using a 56-bit key.
topics.narration:
DES

Data Encryption Standard is a symmetric block cipher using a 56-bit key.

Important exam points. Data Encryption Standard is a symmetric block cipher using a 56-bit key.. Block Size = 64 bits. Key = 56 bits. 16 Rounds. Broken today

topics.title: AES
topics.definition: Advanced Encryption Standard is the modern symmetric encryption standard.
examPoints: Advanced Encryption Standard is the modern symmetric encryption standard.
topics.narration:
AES

Advanced Encryption Standard is the modern symmetric encryption standard.

Important exam points. Advanced Encryption Standard is the modern symmetric encryption standard.. Block Size. 128 bits. Key Sizes. 128. 192. 256. Rounds. 10. 12. 14

topics.title: RC4
topics.definition: RC4 is a stream cipher generating a pseudorandom keystream.
examPoints: RC4 is a stream cipher generating a pseudorandom keystream.
topics.narration:
RC4

RC4 is a stream cipher generating a pseudorandom keystream.

Important exam points. RC4 is a stream cipher generating a pseudorandom keystream.. Stream Cipher. Deprecated. Weak biases

topics.title: RC5
topics.definition: RC5 is a symmetric block cipher with variable block size, key size, and rounds.
examPoints: RC5 is a symmetric block cipher with variable block size, key size, and rounds.
topics.narration:
RC5

RC5 is a symmetric block cipher with variable block size, key size, and rounds.

Important exam points. RC5 is a symmetric block cipher with variable block size, key size, and rounds.. Parameterized Algorithm. More flexible than DES

topics.title: Block Cipher
topics.definition: Encrypts fixed-size blocks of data.
topics.narration:
Block Cipher

Encrypts fixed-size blocks of data.

Important exam points. Encrypts fixed-size blocks of data.. AES. DES. RC5

topics.title: Stream Cipher
topics.definition: Encrypts one bit or byte at a time.
examPoints: Encrypts one bit or byte at a time.
topics.narration:
Stream Cipher

Encrypts one bit or byte at a time.

Important exam points. Encrypts one bit or byte at a time.. RC4

topics.title: Cipher Modes
topics.narration:
Cipher Modes

Important exam points. ECB. CBC. CFB. OFB. CTR. GCM

topics.title: ECB
topics.definition: Encrypts each block independently.
topics.narration:
ECB

Encrypts each block independently.

Important exam points. Encrypts each block independently.. Identical plaintext → Identical ciphertext. Least Secure

topics.title: CBC
topics.definition: Each plaintext block is XORed with previous ciphertext before encryption.
examPoints: Each plaintext block is XORed with previous ciphertext before encryption.
topics.narration:
CBC

Each plaintext block is XORed with previous ciphertext before encryption.

Important exam points. Each plaintext block is XORed with previous ciphertext before encryption.. Needs. IV. More secure than ECB

topics.title: CFB
topics.definition: Converts a block cipher into a stream cipher using previous ciphertext.
examPoints: Converts a block cipher into a stream cipher using previous ciphertext.
topics.narration:
CFB

Converts a block cipher into a stream cipher using previous ciphertext.

Important exam points. Converts a block cipher into a stream cipher using previous ciphertext.. No Padding

topics.title: OFB
topics.definition: Uses previous encryption output instead of ciphertext.
examPoints: Uses previous encryption output instead of ciphertext.
topics.narration:
OFB

Uses previous encryption output instead of ciphertext.

Important exam points. Uses previous encryption output instead of ciphertext.. Transmission errors don't propagate

topics.title: CTR
topics.definition: Uses a counter value encrypted with the key.
examPoints: Uses a counter value encrypted with the key.
topics.narration:
CTR

Uses a counter value encrypted with the key.

Important exam points. Uses a counter value encrypted with the key.. Parallel Processing. Very Fast

topics.title: GCM
topics.definition: AES-GCM provides authenticated encryption.
topics.narration:
GCM

AES-GCM provides authenticated encryption.

Important exam points. AES-GCM provides authenticated encryption.. Confidentiality. Integrity. Most common TLS mode

topics.title: RSA
topics.definition: RSA is an asymmetric algorithm used for encryption, digital signatures, and key exchange.
examPoints: RSA is an asymmetric algorithm used for encryption, digital signatures, and key exchange.
topics.narration:
RSA

RSA is an asymmetric algorithm used for encryption, digital signatures, and key exchange.

Important exam points. RSA is an asymmetric algorithm used for encryption, digital signatures, and key exchange.. Based on. Integer Factorization. Common Keys. 2048. 3072. 4096

topics.title: ECC
topics.definition: Elliptic Curve Cryptography provides equivalent security with much smaller keys.
examPoints: Elliptic Curve Cryptography provides equivalent security with much smaller keys.
topics.narration:
ECC

Elliptic Curve Cryptography provides equivalent security with much smaller keys.

Important exam points. Elliptic Curve Cryptography provides equivalent security with much smaller keys.. RSA 3072 ≈ ECC P-256. Faster. Smaller Keys

topics.title: RSA vs ECC
comparisons: RSA: Large Keys; ECC: Small Keys
topics.narration:
RSA vs ECC

Comparison. RSA: Large Keys; ECC: Small Keys. RSA: Slower; ECC: Faster. RSA: Factorization; ECC: Elliptic Curves

topics.title: Diffie-Hellman
topics.definition: A key exchange algorithm allowing two parties to establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.
examPoints: A key exchange algorithm allowing two parties to establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.
topics.narration:
Diffie-Hellman

A key exchange algorithm allowing two parties to establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.

Important exam points. A key exchange algorithm allowing two parties to establish a shared secret over an insecure channel.. Does NOT encrypt data. Does NOT authenticate users

topics.title: ECDH
topics.definition: Elliptic Curve version of Diffie-Hellman.
topics.narration:
ECDH

Elliptic Curve version of Diffie-Hellman.

Important exam points. Elliptic Curve version of Diffie-Hellman.. Smaller Keys. Faster

topics.title: Hash Function
topics.definition: A one-way mathematical function converting data into a fixed-length digest.
examPoints: A one-way mathematical function converting data into a fixed-length digest.
topics.narration:
Hash Function

A one-way mathematical function converting data into a fixed-length digest.

Important exam points. A one-way mathematical function converting data into a fixed-length digest.. Integrity. Not Encryption

topics.title: Properties of Secure Hash
topics.narration:
Properties of Secure Hash

Important exam points. Fixed Length. One-Way. Collision Resistant. Avalanche Effect

topics.title: Collision Resistance
topics.definition: It should be computationally infeasible to find two different inputs producing the same hash.
examPoints: It should be computationally infeasible to find two different inputs producing the same hash.
topics.narration:
Collision Resistance

It should be computationally infeasible to find two different inputs producing the same hash.

Important exam points. It should be computationally infeasible to find two different inputs producing the same hash.. Important for Digital Signatures

topics.title: Avalanche Effect
topics.definition: A small change in input produces a completely different hash output.
examPoints: A small change in input produces a completely different hash output.
topics.narration:
Avalanche Effect

A small change in input produces a completely different hash output.

Important exam points. A small change in input produces a completely different hash output.. Desired Property

topics.title: SHA Family
topics.narration:
SHA Family

Important exam points. MD5. SHA-1

Comparison. Algorithm: MD5; Status: Broken. Algorithm: SHA-1; Status: Broken. Algorithm: SHA-224; Status: Secure. Algorithm: SHA-256; Status: Secure. Algorithm: SHA-384; Status: Secure. Algorithm: SHA-512; Status: Secure. Algorithm: SHA-3; Status: Latest Standard

topics.title: HMAC
topics.definition: Hash-based Message Authentication Code combines a secret key with a hash function.
examPoints: Hash-based Message Authentication Code combines a secret key with a hash function.
topics.narration:
HMAC

Hash-based Message Authentication Code combines a secret key with a hash function.

Important exam points. Hash-based Message Authentication Code combines a secret key with a hash function.. Integrity. Authentication. Formula

topics.title: Digital Signature
topics.definition: A Digital Signature uses the sender's private key to sign a hash of the message. The receiver verifies it using the sender's public key.
examPoints: A Digital Signature uses the sender's private key to sign a hash of the message. The receiver verifies it using the sender's public key.
topics.narration:
Digital Signature

A Digital Signature uses the sender's private key to sign a hash of the message. The receiver verifies it using the sender's public key.

Important exam points. A Digital Signature uses the sender's private key to sign a hash of the message. The receiver verifies it using the sender's public key.. Authentication. Integrity. Non-Repudiation. Does NOT provide Confidentiality

topics.title: Digital Signature vs Encryption
comparisons: Digital Signature: Private Key; Encryption: Public Key
Digital Signature: Public Key verifies; Encryption: Private Key decrypts
topics.narration:
Digital Signature vs Encryption

Comparison. Digital Signature: Private Key; Encryption: Public Key. Digital Signature: Public Key verifies; Encryption: Private Key decrypts. Digital Signature: Authentication; Encryption: Confidentiality

topics.title: Cryptanalysis
topics.definition: Cryptanalysis is the study of breaking cryptographic systems without knowing the secret key.
examPoints: Cryptanalysis is the study of breaking cryptographic systems without knowing the secret key.
topics.narration:
Cryptanalysis

Cryptanalysis is the study of breaking cryptographic systems without knowing the secret key.

Important exam points. Cryptanalysis is the study of breaking cryptographic systems without knowing the secret key.. Recover. Plaintext. Key

topics.title: Brute Force Attack
topics.definition: Attempts every possible key until the correct one is found.
examPoints: Attempts every possible key until the correct one is found.
topics.narration:
Brute Force Attack

Attempts every possible key until the correct one is found.

Important exam points. Attempts every possible key until the correct one is found.. Defense. Large Key Size

topics.title: Dictionary Attack
topics.definition: Uses common passwords or words instead of every possible combination.
examPoints: Uses common passwords or words instead of every possible combination.
topics.narration:
Dictionary Attack

Uses common passwords or words instead of every possible combination.

Important exam points. Uses common passwords or words instead of every possible combination.. Common against Passwords

topics.title: Birthday Attack
topics.definition: Exploits collisions in hash functions.
topics.narration:
Birthday Attack

Exploits collisions in hash functions.

Important exam points. Exploits collisions in hash functions.. Targets. Hash Algorithms

topics.title: Known Plaintext Attack
topics.definition: Attacker knows both plaintext and ciphertext.
examPoints: Attacker knows both plaintext and ciphertext.
topics.narration:
Known Plaintext Attack

Attacker knows both plaintext and ciphertext.

Important exam points. Attacker knows both plaintext and ciphertext.. Attempts to recover the key.

topics.title: Chosen Plaintext Attack
topics.definition: Attacker encrypts chosen plaintext and studies the ciphertext.
examPoints: Attacker encrypts chosen plaintext and studies the ciphertext.
topics.narration:
Chosen Plaintext Attack

Attacker encrypts chosen plaintext and studies the ciphertext.

Important exam points. Attacker encrypts chosen plaintext and studies the ciphertext.. Common in cryptographic analysis.

topics.title: Side Channel Attack
topics.definition: Uses physical information such as timing, power consumption, or electromagnetic leakage.
examPoints: Uses physical information such as timing, power consumption, or electromagnetic leakage.
topics.narration:
Side Channel Attack

Uses physical information such as timing, power consumption, or electromagnetic leakage.

Important exam points. Uses physical information such as timing, power consumption, or electromagnetic leakage.. Timing Attack. Power Analysis. Cache Attack

topics.title: Phase 2 - Cryptography
examPoints: (No need to memorize the math deeply-just know it establishes the same shared secret.)
topics.narration:
Phase 2 - Cryptography

Important exam points. (No need to memorize the math deeply-just know it establishes the same shared secret.)

topics.title: Top 25 One-Liners for Exam
oneLiners: Digital Signature provides Authentication + Integrity + Non-Repudiation.
ECB is the least secure mode.
RSA security is based on integer factorization.
ECC security is based on the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP).
Diffie-Hellman performs key exchange, not encryption.
ECDH is the elliptic-curve version of Diffie-Hellman.
SHA-256 is the most commonly used secure hash.
Avalanche Effect means a tiny input change causes a drastically different hash.
Collision Resistance means it is impractical to find two inputs with the same hash.
Brute-force attacks target keys; dictionary attacks target passwords.
TLS commonly uses RSA/ECDHE for key exchange and AES-GCM for secure data encryption.
topics.narration:
Top 25 One-Liners for Exam

Memory points. Encryption provides Confidentiality.. Hashing provides Integrity.. HMAC provides Integrity + Authentication.. Digital Signature provides Authentication + Integrity + Non-Repudiation.. RSA is asymmetric.. AES is symmetric.. DES key = 56 bits.. AES block size = 128 bits.. AES keys = 128/192/256 bits.. RC4 is a Stream Cipher.. AES is a Block Cipher.. ECB is the least secure mode.. CBC requires an IV.. CTR supports parallel encryption.. GCM provides authenticated encryption.. RSA security is based on integer factorization.. ECC security is based on the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP).. Diffie-Hellman performs key exchange, not encryption.. ECDH is the elliptic-curve version of Diffie-Hellman.. MD5 and SHA-1 are broken.. SHA-256 is the most commonly used secure hash.. Avalanche Effect means a tiny input change causes a drastically different hash.. Collision Resistance means it is impractical to find two inputs with the same hash.. Brute-force attacks target keys; dictionary attacks target passwords.. TLS commonly uses RSA/ECDHE for key exchange and AES-GCM for secure data encryption.
